page: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a 7559 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are present on this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D128lds_v7 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving high marks in stress-ng multi-core tests and PassMark CPU suites. Its memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, particularly in large-block transfers. Cryptographic and compression operations, such as bzip3 decompression and PassMark encryption tests, also rank in the top decile. Database performance is notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 196.23 million operations per second and static web serving throughput peaking at 145.81 GB/sec. Single-core performance and text generation speeds for large language models fall into the average tier.

Given its high compute density paired with a relatively low memory footprint, the D128lds_v7 offers a specialized cost-efficiency profile. It is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory allocations per thread. Tradeoffs include a lower memory-to-core ratio, which may limit memory-bound applications. The instance is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded batch processing, database operations utilizing local storage, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a lean 2.0 GB of memory per core, and is bundled with 7559 GB of local storage. The instance excels in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations, placing in the top tier of benchmarks. It achieves 196.23 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 145.81 GB/sec in static web serving. Lacking a GPU, it relies on Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions for accelerated vector and matrix math. This server is optimized for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and compute-intensive batch processing where high CPU density is required but memory demands are moderate.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, 256 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, designed for high-throughput database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, built on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 128 vCPUs, fast local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz.
256 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio.
7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based inference.
